title: Transfer Stations
summary: Transfer stations are drop-off sites where waste and recyclable materials are collected before being moved to another facility.
description: Transfer stations are commonly used by councils and regional communities as central drop-off points. They may accept mixed household waste, bulky waste, green waste, scrap metal, cardboard, e-waste or other recyclable materials depending on the site. Materials are usually sorted, consolidated and transferred to recycling, recovery, treatment or disposal facilities.
avoidPuttingIn: Batteries unless a battery collection point is provided
Chemicals unless accepted by the facility
Asbestos unless the site is approved for it
Liquids or hazardous waste unless listed as accepted
userTip: Use the location details before travelling because transfer stations can differ greatly in what they accept.

title: E-waste Drop-off and Recycling
summary: E-waste facilities accept electronic items that should not be placed in general waste.
description: E-waste includes electrical and electronic products that are no longer wanted or working. These items can contain valuable recoverable materials such as metals and plastics, but they may also contain hazardous components. E-waste drop-off facilities may only collect items, while recycling facilities may dismantle, sort or process them.
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether batteries must be removed first
Whether data should be erased from phones or computers
Whether the site accepts business/commercial e-waste
avoidPuttingIn: Devices containing personal data without wiping them
Broken screens without checking handling requirements
Large appliances if the site only accepts small e-waste
userTip: Before dropping off phones, laptops or storage devices, remove personal data and factory reset the device where possible.

title: Container Deposit Drop-off
databaseCategory: Drop-off - Container deposit scheme drop-off facility
summary: Container deposit facilities collect eligible bottles and cans through refund schemes.
description: Container deposit facilities are linked to refund schemes for eligible drink containers. These facilities help recover aluminium, glass, plastic and liquid paperboard containers. Some sites are automated depots, while others are staffed collection points.
avoidPuttingIn: Wine or spirit bottles if not accepted by the local scheme
userTip: Check container eligibility before travelling so you do not bring items that the refund point cannot accept.

title: Organics Recycling
summary: Organics recycling facilities recover green waste, garden waste and other organic materials.
description: Organics recycling facilities process organic materials into useful products such as mulch, compost or soil conditioners. These facilities help divert biodegradable material from landfill and support resource recovery.
userTip: Keep organic material free from plastic, metal and general waste because contamination can make recovery harder.

title: Construction and Demolition Waste
summary: C&D facilities recover materials from building, renovation and demolition waste.
description: Construction and demolition waste facilities process building materials so they can be reused, recycled or recovered. These facilities may sort mixed loads and recover materials such as concrete, bricks, timber, metals and rubble.
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether the site accepts mixed or separated loads
Fees by weight or vehicle type
avoidPuttingIn: Asbestos unless the site is approved for it
userTip: Separate materials before drop-off where possible because clean separated loads are often easier to recycle.

title: Metals Recovery
summary: Metals recovery facilities collect and process metal items for recycling.
description: Metals recovery facilities recover ferrous and non-ferrous metals from scrap items. These facilities may accept metal parts, tools, appliances, steel, aluminium and other metal products depending on the operator.
commonItems: Vehicle or machinery parts where accepted
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether non-metal parts must be removed
avoidPuttingIn: Electronic items unless the site also accepts e-waste
userTip: Check if the facility accepts appliances or only clean scrap metal before travelling.

title: Materials Recovery Facilities
summary: Materials recovery facilities sort mixed recyclable materials into separate streams.
description: Materials recovery facilities, often called MRFs, sort recyclable materials from mixed recycling streams. They may separate paper, cardboard, plastics, metals and glass so these materials can be sent for further processing.
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether the facility accepts direct public drop-off
Whether materials need to be sorted first
userTip: Do not assume every MRF accepts public drop-off; some only process council or commercial recycling streams.

title: Paper and Cardboard Recycling
databaseCategory: Recycling - Paper and cardboard recycling facility
summary: Paper and cardboard facilities recover clean paper-based materials.
description: Paper and cardboard recycling facilities recover fibre-based materials for reprocessing. Clean, dry and uncontaminated material is usually easier to recycle than material with food, grease or liquid contamination.
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether paper and cardboard must be separated
avoidPuttingIn: Greasy pizza boxes if heavily contaminated
Waxed or laminated materials unless accepted
userTip: Flatten boxes and keep paper/cardboard dry to improve recyclability.

title: Reuse Shops
summary: Reuse shops redirect usable items away from landfill.
description: Reuse shops collect, sell or redistribute items that are still usable. They support waste avoidance by extending the life of products before recycling or disposal becomes necessary.
userTip: Only donate items that are clean, safe and reusable.

title: Hazardous Waste Facilities
summary: Hazardous waste facilities manage materials that need special handling.
description: Hazardous waste facilities may treat, recycle, store, transfer or dispose of waste that presents safety, health or environmental risks. These facilities may not be suitable for general public drop-off and may operate under strict acceptance rules.
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether the public can use the facility
avoidPuttingIn: Explosive or highly reactive materials unless specifically accepted
userTip: Contact the facility before travelling because hazardous waste sites often have strict acceptance and safety requirements.

title: Landfill
summary: Landfill is for waste that cannot be reused, recycled or recovered.
description: Landfill facilities dispose of residual waste that is not suitable for recovery. While landfill is necessary for some waste streams, EcoDrop Finder encourages users to check reuse, recycling and recovery options first.
checkBeforeYouGo: Whether the item can be recycled elsewhere
userTip: Treat landfill as the last option after checking whether the item can be reused, recycled or recovered.
